marked_piece_destination_count: {standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} How many empty squares can the marked piece move to in one move?
{standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} Count the empty destination squares the marked piece can move to in one move.
{standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} Using normal chess movement, how many empty destination squares are available to that piece?
{standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} Determine the number of empty one-move destination squares for the marked piece.
{standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} How many empty legal movement destinations does the marked piece have?
marked_piece_capture_count: {standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} How many opponent pieces can the marked piece capture in one move?
{standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} Count the opponent pieces the marked piece can capture.
{standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} Using normal chess movement, how many opponent pieces are capturable by that piece?
{standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} Determine the number of opponent pieces the marked piece can capture.
{standard_rule_text} {marked_piece_rule_text} How many enemy pieces can the marked piece take in one move?
player_capture_piece_count: {standard_rule_text} How many {opponent_color_name} pieces can {player_color_name} capture in one move?
{standard_rule_text} Count the {opponent_color_name} pieces that are capturable by {player_color_name} in one move.
How many opponent pieces are currently capturable by {player_color_name}? {standard_rule_text}
{standard_rule_text} Determine how many {opponent_color_name} pieces can be captured by any {player_color_name} piece in one move.
{standard_rule_text} For {player_color_name}, count the opponent pieces that can be taken immediately.
king_square_attacker_count: {standard_rule_text} {king_square_attacker_rule_text} How many opponent pieces are attacking the marked king?
{king_square_attacker_rule_text} Count the opponent pieces giving check to the marked king.
{standard_rule_text} {king_square_attacker_rule_text} Determine how many enemy pieces attack that king.
{king_square_attacker_rule_text} How many opponent pieces currently threaten the marked king?
{standard_rule_text} {king_square_attacker_rule_text} Count the pieces attacking the marked king.
white_piece_attacks_target_square_count: {standard_rule_text} {target_square_rule_text} How many white pieces attack the target square?
{target_square_rule_text} How many white pieces attack that square?
{standard_rule_text} Determine how many white pieces attack the marked target square.
How many white pieces currently attack the red outlined target square?
{target_square_rule_text} Count the white pieces attacking that target square.
black_piece_attacks_target_square_count: {standard_rule_text} {target_square_rule_text} How many black pieces attack the target square?
{target_square_rule_text} How many black pieces attack that square?
{standard_rule_text} Determine how many black pieces attack the marked target square.
How many black pieces currently attack the red outlined target square?
{target_square_rule_text} Count the black pieces attacking that target square.
king_escape_square_count: {standard_rule_text} {king_escape_rule_text} How many safe squares can the marked king move to?
{king_escape_rule_text} Count the safe one-step destinations for the marked king.
{standard_rule_text} {king_escape_rule_text} Determine the number of legal escape squares for that king.
{king_escape_rule_text} How many destination squares can the marked king move to without being attacked?
{standard_rule_text} {king_escape_rule_text} Count the safe movement destinations for the marked king.
piece_kind_count: How many {target_piece_kind_plural} are on the board?
How many chess {target_piece_kind_plural} are on the board?
Count all {target_piece_kind_plural} shown on the board.
colored_piece_kind_count: How many {target_color_name} {target_piece_kind_plural} are on the board?
How many {target_color_name} chess {target_piece_kind_plural} are on the board?
Count all {target_color_name} {target_piece_kind_plural} shown on the board.
checkmate_move_label: {standard_rule_text} {coordinate_rule_text} {checkmate_rule_text} {player_color_name} to move. Which labeled move checkmates {defender_color_name} immediately?
{coordinate_rule_text} {standard_rule_text} {player_color_name} moves now. Which option gives immediate checkmate against {defender_color_name}?
{standard_rule_text} {coordinate_rule_text} Choose the labeled move that checkmates {defender_color_name} in one move.
{coordinate_rule_text} {checkmate_rule_text} Which candidate move is an immediate checkmate for {player_color_name}?
{standard_rule_text} {player_color_name} to move. Using the coordinate labels, choose the option that checkmates {defender_color_name} now.

scene:visible_chess_board.object_description: a partial chess board with white and black pieces

query:marked_piece_destination_count.standard_rule_text: Use normal chess piece movement for one move only. Do not use castling, en passant, or promotion. Friendly pieces block movement; opponent pieces are capture targets and are not empty destinations.
query:marked_piece_destination_count.marked_piece_rule_text: The red outlined square contains the marked piece.
query:marked_piece_destination_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of empty destination squares for the marked piece
query:marked_piece_destination_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space square boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every empty destination square the marked piece can move to; use an empty array if none are available

query:marked_piece_capture_count.standard_rule_text: Use normal chess piece movement for one move only. Do not use castling, en passant, or promotion. Friendly pieces block movement; count only opponent-occupied squares the marked piece can capture.
query:marked_piece_capture_count.marked_piece_rule_text: The red outlined square contains the marked piece.
query:marked_piece_capture_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of opponent pieces the marked piece can capture in one move
query:marked_piece_capture_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space square boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every capturable destination square occupied by an opponent piece; use an empty array if none are available

query:player_capture_piece_count.standard_rule_text: Use normal chess piece movement. Do not use castling, en passant, or promotion.
query:player_capture_piece_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of opponent pieces capturable in one move by the named side
query:player_capture_piece_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space piece boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every capturable opponent piece; use an empty array if none are available

query:king_square_attacker_count.standard_rule_text: Use normal chess piece movement. Do not use castling, en passant, or promotion.
query:king_square_attacker_count.king_square_attacker_rule_text: The red outlined square contains the marked king.
query:king_square_attacker_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of opponent pieces attacking the marked king
query:king_square_attacker_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space piece boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every attacking opponent piece; use an empty array if none are attacking

query:white_piece_attacks_target_square_.standard_rule_text: Use normal chess piece movement. Do not use castling, en passant, or promotion.
query:white_piece_attacks_target_square_.target_square_rule_text: The red outlined square marks the target square.
query:white_piece_attacks_target_square_.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of white pieces attacking the marked target square
query:white_piece_attacks_target_square_.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space piece boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every white attacking piece; use an empty array if none are attacking

query:black_piece_attacks_target_square_.standard_rule_text: Use normal chess piece movement. Do not use castling, en passant, or promotion.
query:black_piece_attacks_target_square_.target_square_rule_text: The red outlined square marks the target square.
query:black_piece_attacks_target_square_.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of black pieces attacking the marked target square
query:black_piece_attacks_target_square_.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space piece boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every black attacking piece; use an empty array if none are attacking

query:king_escape_square_count.standard_rule_text: Use normal chess piece movement. Do not use castling, en passant, or promotion.
query:king_escape_square_count.king_escape_rule_text: The red outlined square contains the marked king. Count one-step king destinations that are empty or capturable and are not attacked after the king moves there.
query:king_escape_square_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of safe one-step destination squares for the marked king
query:king_escape_square_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space square boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every safe destination square; use an empty array if there are none

query:piece_kind_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of matching visible pieces
query:piece_kind_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space piece boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every matching visible piece; use an empty array if none are shown

query:colored_piece_kind_count.answer_hint: set "answer" to the count of matching visible pieces
query:colored_piece_kind_count.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON array of pixel-space piece boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every matching visible piece; use an empty array if none are shown

query:checkmate_move_label.standard_rule_text: Use normal chess piece movement. Do not use castling, en passant, or promotion.
query:checkmate_move_label.coordinate_rule_text: Board coordinates are shown on the margins, with files A-H left to right and ranks 1-8 bottom to top.
query:checkmate_move_label.checkmate_rule_text: A move checkmates when it attacks the opposing king and that side has no legal move to escape.
query:checkmate_move_label.answer_hint: set "answer" to the single selected option letter shown in the move-option panel
query:checkmate_move_label.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to a JSON object with keys "from", "to", and "king", each mapped to a pixel-space point [x, y] at the center of the selected move's source square, destination square, and opposing king square
